title: Restore EvaluationTask related resource timestamp layout
description:
EvaluationTask detail related-resource cards regressed: the link icon moved into the right metadata slot and the right-side timestamp disappeared.

Behavior spec:
Feature: EvaluationTask related resource metadata
Scenario: Procedure and score version links show stable metadata
Given an EvaluationTask is shown in detail mode with a linked procedure and score version
When the related resource cards render
Then each card shows its link icon inline next to the linked identifier/title
And each card shows its relative timestamp in the right-side metadata area
And both timestamps use the shared Timestamp component styling.

Definition of Done:
- Procedure and Score Version RelatedResourceCard usages keep inline link icons.
- Right-side timestamps render through a shared timestamp prop/path.
- Focused EvaluationTask tests cover the layout.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting fix on current branch. Scope is limited to RelatedResourceCard/EvaluationTask behavior: keep inline link icons next to the related resource identity while rendering timestamps through the shared right-side Timestamp path.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented local fix: RelatedResourceCard now has a rightTimestamp prop that renders the shared Timestamp component in the right metadata area while leaving inlineLink placement unchanged. EvaluationTask Procedure and Score Version cards now pass rightTimestamp instead of duplicate rightMeta Timestamp JSX. Regression test verifies both related-resource links are not inside the right metadata container and that two right-side Timestamp components render. Verification: npm test -- EvaluationTask.category-filter.test.tsx passed with existing act warnings.
